Hidden files not handled correctly (1FWHT63)

There is a problem wrt how java.io.File surfaces hidden files.  ; For a hidden file; 	File.exists() => true; 	File.isFile() => false; 	File.isDirectory() => false; For a hidden directory; 	File.exists() => true; 	File.isFile() => false; 	File.isDirectory() => true; ; As a result; the workbench gets confused when refreshing against the local ; disk.  The ; hidden files show up in the File.list() result but then we try isFile and ; isDirectory; and both return false for the hidden files.  ; ; Some of the workbench code uses the pattern; 	if (file.isFile()); 		<do file stuff>; 	else; 		<do dir stuff>; In the case of hidden files we end up doing the <dir stuff>.  ; ; This problem must be addressed across the board by ensuring that hidden files; are either ignored or not ignored.  Which ever choice is picked; the policy must; be consistently applied.; ; NOTES:; ; JM(6/13/2000 12:58:35 PM); 	This change is somewhat important as it surfaces when the user is using ; Word etc.; 	Word creates a hidden temp file when opening a .doc.  Currently this is ; handled; 	inconsistently.  Ideally the hidden files would be ignored.; ; JW (6/13/00 12:58:04 PM); 	Given the information above; I see how you can detect a hidden file; ; but not a ; 	hidden directory. My inclination would be to ignore hidden files (= act ; like they dont; 	exist to the workbench); ; JM (6/20/2000 1:54:33 PM); 	Deferred until after the tech preview.  This change is too risky now.; ; JM (08/02/2001 10:41:08 PM); 	Reopening and moving to candidates; ; JM (4/16/2001 11:17:54 PM); 	Moving to RTP for investigation. When you try to store a large object in the index store you get a walkback ; which is less than ; easy to understand.  We need to fail with more information here.  ; ; NOTES:; ; JM (4/24/2001 12:33:44 PM); 	See also:; 		1GCI4PD: ITPJCORE:ALL - Exception setting Java project classpath; 	The API specs that you should not store properites larger than 2k.  ; When the user tries to do this; ; 	perhaps we should stop earlier?  This would avoid the problem all ; together.  Better would ; 	be to allow the storage of things which will fit  but fail ; intelligently when they dont.; ; dwd (5/21/2001 4:02:50 PM) ; You can store long values in an index by chopping them up into sections and ; storing them in multiple; adjacent keys.  For example; the suppose you have a (key; value) pair to be ; inserted.  Generate new keys; of the form key.00; ... key.99 and pair them with values v00; ...; v99 where ; v00 is bytes 0 to 1999 of the ; original value and v01 is bytes 2000 to 3999 of the original value; etc.; ; An alternative is to store the value as a blob -- the size limit is 7K+; then ; then store the ObjectID returned; as the value of the key.  ObjectIDs are guaranteed to never be reused.  They ; are an open number; ; serial number pair.; ; If the object is very large; you can store it as a separate file and store the ; file name as the value.; ; RTP (6/2/01 2:37:21 PM); 	Moving to DD for investigation.; ; JM (6/5/2001 12:39:34 PM); 	There are several workarounds for this issue and the API works as ; specd.  Suggest we ; 		- ensure that the failure mode is reasonable; 		- defer further work until after June.; ; dwd (6/5/2001 11:45:57 AM); Agreed.  Moving to deferred.

Different spellings for character encodings (1FU2J20)

Java OutputStreamWriter expects the UTF-8 character encoding; 	string constant to be UTF8 while the mod-dav xml parser expects; 	utf-8. This causes problems when the character encoding is set; 	based on whats in the context on outgoing requests to the server.; 	See Request.writeDocumentBody for the temporary workaround for; 	this problem.; ; NOTES:

EC: Bug in disposal of JFace parts: WorkbenchPage.hideView(IViewPart) (1GLEI38)

I agree it is confusing and I think for consistency we should consider having the ContentOutline explicitly ; dispose of its pages control before calling dispose on the page. ; ; ; ; Randy Hudson <none@ibm.com> wrote in message news:9q2ci0$p80$1@rogue.oti.com...; > That is very confusing.  Sometimes dispose() means that a part should; > dispose its control; sometimes it doesnt.  Also; if I were interested in; > the state of my control when my Part is disposed; I cannot check its state; > in my parts dispose method.  So; I have to hook a dispose listener onto my; > part; and I also have to conditionally dispose my control in my parts; > dispose() method.; > ; > What is the benefit of having these two code paths be different?  Is there a; > case where it makes sense to dispose the Parts control; and then dispose the; > part afterwards?; > ; > Randy Giffen <none@oti.com> wrote in message; > news:9q2957$nem$1@rogue.oti.com...; > > It is by design that WorkbenchPage disposes of the container for its part; > > controls before calling dispose() on the parts. This is the last method; > that; > > the workbench page will ever call on its parts and the parts may use this; > > dispose method to clean up allocated resources.; > >; > > ContentOutline (a part) forwards its dispose() call to its content outline; > > pages and in this case the pages control is already disposed. In; > addition;; > > ContentOutline calls dispose() on a content outline page if the editor; > which; > > contributed that page is closed. In this case the page is responsible for; > > disposing its control.; > >; > ; > ; > ; ; NOTES:

Entering tabs as spaces (1GL3BDU)

A good idea from the eclipse.tools newsgroup:; ; Ive just started working in a project that requires all files to have ; spaces rather than any tabs. I personally agree that this is a good ; thing. With my previous editor (a plain Java-aware text editor) I didnt ; need to do anything fancy for this; as I could set it to do everything ; using spaces anyway; and pressing tab just put the cursor to the ; right place on the line rather than inserting a normal tab character.; ; Im okay with tabbing to the right place on the line using Eclipse; but ; Id love it if (either at save time or key entry time) it could convert ; the tabs into spaces. Is there any way to do this? I know that the code ; formatter is capable of using spaces rather than tabs for indentation; ; but it doesnt seem to apply that to hand-entered tabs.; ; Jon Skeet - <skeet@pobox.com>; ; ; We should support entering tabs as spaces and allow the user to; specify the number of spaces to use as well. ; NOTES:

Need to change the way the cell editors behave in Properties and Tasks views (1GL3C6Z)

It appears that we need to change the way the cell editors in the Tasks and Properties views; behave when the view is deactivated.; ; Related to 1GJUNL6: ITPUI:ALL - EC: PropertySheetViewer.setInput() does not save editor value.; This is sort of a follow up problem to 1GJUNL6; ; The problem is that when you edit a field in the Properties view (or the the description in the Tasks view) ; then you click on something else (another program; somewhere else in the workbench - doesnt matter ; if it is another part; an editor; the toolbar; or whatever) the edited value should be accepted as the ; new value AS SOON AS you click on that something else.  I guess that means as soon as the part is ; deactivated; the value should be accepted.; ; Example 1:; In the Tasks view; bring up the context menu and create a new task; Click on the Description field to edit; Type some text to change the description; Click somewhere else; for example on the editor or the Navigator.; 	Also try clicking on another open program; like Netscape or the file Explorer.; Click on the title bar of the Tasks view; Hit Esc key; BUG: The new value you typed in step 3 should have been accepted when ; you clicked somewhere else in step 4; ; Example 2:; Install the property sheet example by loading the project org.eclipse.ui.propertysheet; 	from the desktop repo.; Run your target.; ; In the target:; -Create a file called me.usr; open the file in an editor; -Open the Outline and Properties views if not already open; -In the Outline; expand nodes until you see first names; click on richard; -In the Properties view; expand the address category.  Click on the city field to ;  edit.  Enter Ottawa as the new city; -Click somewhere else; for example the editor (the Nav or the Outline are bad examples;  of part to click in this case since set input on the Properties view).  Also try clicking on another ;  open program; like Netscape or the file Explorer.; -Click the title bar of the Properties view; -Hit Esc; BUG: the value was not accepted when the Properties view lost focus; ; NOTES:

Preference dialog disappears when pref page has TabFolder (1GL4MPR)

Possible problem dealing with multiple plugins with same id/version (1GF6L8K)

I understand that duplicate plugins should not exist in the system; but it is possible; 	that during plugin development; different plugins with the same version and id; 	may be loaded into the plugin registry.  This has happened to me while working on some; 	SWT examples.  The latest build copy of the plugins was in dev\eclipse\plugins\...; and; 	the new development edition was in target\eclipse\plugins\....  So when PDE started up; 	(self-hosting Eclipse would do the same thing); both copies were included in the; 	registry.; ; 	The differences between the two versions were:; 		- some code changes (no new classes); 		- one additional dependency; ; 	It is the additional dependency that caused problems.  When trying to reference classes; 	introduced into the plugins runtime environment via the new dependency; the ClassLoader; 	failed to find them - issuing a ClassDefNotFoundError.  Some other minor quirks as well; 	in the data fetched from the PluginRegistry (the new dependency figured on both copies of; 	the plugin; not just the second).; ; 	Though this problem should never occur in practice; it may be helpful to add a couple lines; 	to either:; 		1) discard plugins with the same id and version as one already in the registry; 		2) disambiguate seeming duplicates by appending an extra character to the version; 		    number of those plugin id / version pairs that get registred twice so the; 			assertions about there being only one plugin with a given id and version holds.; ; 	JB (11/06/2001 8:16:28 PM); ; NOTES:; ; JM (6/12/2001 3:07:20 PM); 	defer

[resources] consider limiting total size of history store (1GF603Z)

There is no API way of limiting the total disk space taken by the Local History Store.; ; NOTES:; ; dwd (6/12/2001 4:42:46 PM); ; History stores are large because much of the space of index nodes is contained in keys that are; almost exactly alike.  The key/value data in the nodes could be compressed (using gzip) and split among ; several relatively small newly defined blob segment objects.  This way; the storage used by a history would; be dramatically reduced.; ; An API should also be defined limiting the size of a particular index.  An exception or status could be returned; if an insert is attempted that would cause an index to violate its limit.  A number of entries limit would be easy to ; enforce.  A byte limit would be harder.

[resources] History Store is leaking (1GF9ZLP)

You probably; create these something like this:; ; Index index = store.createIndex(index);; ...; ObjectID id = store.createObject(bsob);; index.insert(key; id);; ...; ; ; When you remove an index entry (perhaps youve hit a limit and are cleaning up the store); you probably do something like:; ; IndexCursor cursor = index.open();; cursor.find(name);; cursor.remove();; ; what I think this should be is:; ; cursor.find(name);; id = cursor.getValueAsObjectID();; store.removeObject(id);; cursor.remove();; ; NOTES:; ; RTP (6/13/01 4:11:26 PM); 	The right thing is done in the Property Store but the History Store does not; delete the BSOB; just the index entry.; ; RTP (6/13/01 4:10:43 PM); 	Moving to Deferred.; ; RTP (6/13/01 4:44:37 PM); 	Similiar problems detected in the property store when updating values.; From DD:; You could be getting the same effect if you are updating an index entry to point to a new object eg.; ; cursor.find(key);; ObjectID newID = store.createObject(newStuff);; cursor.updateValue(newID);; ; should be:; ; cursor.find(key);; ObjectID oldID = cursor.getValueAsObjectID();; store.removeObject(oldID);; ObjectID newID = store.createObject(newStuff);; cursor.updateValue(newID);; ; Could you be doing that when you update a property?; ; RTP (6/13/01 4:45:14 PM); 	Thats exactly what we are doing.

[resources] Cut down the size of indexed stores (1GFA3XN)

We need to find some ways to make the footprint of indexed stores smaller.  One way would be to compress; the data in an index node since much of the data is duplicated from entry to entry.  Another would be to decrease ; the page size; but that would require a complicated conversion and the implementation of BLOBs.; ; ; NOTES:

[resources] Implement BLOBs in indexed stores (1GFA416)

Currently objects are limited in size to 6K; since they need to fit inside a page.  Find a way to make these; larger; perhaps by chopping them up into blob segments that can be assembled on demand.  An API addition ; may be needed to allow streaming over these objects since copying them may be too demanding on memory.; ; Blobs could also be used as values in indices.; ; NOTES:

Ant Javac task uses the execution runtime to compile (1GF0S3C)

The Javac task (actually the DefaultCompilerAdapter) by default uses the current VMs java.home as the base; for finding the class lib to compile against. Invalid characters in file names.; 	2). File name case sensitivity.; 	3). Reserved words in file names.; 	4). File name length.; 	5). File name segment depth.; ; NOTES:; ; --------------------------------------------; 1). ; ; --------------------------------------------; 2).; ; Notes from referenced PRs:; ; The problem might be solved if we are using force = false but with force = true it might be worse.; We could have two (or more) different files in the workspace writing to the same file in the file system.; (e.g. test.java and Test.java); ; RefreshLocal is also affected. Need to decide if when refreshing a resource that has an existing; location on disk; we throw an exception to warn the user or simply ignore.; ; 	Given the scenario:; 		Workspace contains:; 			Abc; 		File system contains:; 			Abc; xyz; ; 	Usecases and expected behaviour (on a non case sensitive FS):; 	1) Rename Abc to abc => OK; 	2) Create ABC => FAIL; 	3) Create XYZ => FAIL; 	4) Rename (in FS) Abc to abc + Refresh Local => DELETE Abc + CREATE abc; ; Also:; 	Potential problem areas:; 	- property store; 	- history store; 	- persisted markers and sync info; 	- resource lookup in the tree; ; --------------------------------------------; 3).; ; Notes from referenced PRs:; ; The following directory names are not allowed by Win98 or WinNT:; 	con; com1; com2; ...; com9; lpt1; lpt2; ...; lpt9.  ; Checking for existence on these files always returns true.; ; --------------------------------------------; 4).; --------------------------------------------; 5).; --------------------------------------------; ; DJH (5/15/01 4:03:04 PM); ; Action items/issues:; ; - UnifiedTree -> fix the merge code for workspace/filesystem resources; - how is #resourceFor affected by case-sensitivity on a case insensitive FS?; - create/move/copy -> existence checks; 	- for example; for create:; 		- create force false all the time and if there is a problem and force is true then; 			check the names to see if we are ok; - case sensitivity information should be available on a per project basis since; 	project content can occur across different filesystems.; - must be able to rename/change file casing (e.g. foo.java -> Foo.java); - #setContents -> if someone changes the casing from underneath you; just set; 	the content as per normal and the old resource will be removed/the new resource; 	will be added during the next refresh local. Yes; this will be treated as an add/delete; 	and remove properties; markers; etc.; - need a story for path validity. where does it happen? At the API level or below?; ; RTP (5/29/01 9:48:39 PM); ; High priority:; 	1) Need to be able to rename a resource changing only the case.; 		1.1) Should handle using API and file system; 	2) Create a resource (force TRUE) but a resource with different case exists on disk.; 		The problem is to end up with two resources having the same location on disk.; ; Medium priority:; 	1) Create a resource (force FALSE) but a resource with different case exists on disk.; 		1.1) We should not create the resource and should find the existing one.; 	2) Change a file case on disk and set the contents (keepHistory FALSE).

[runtime] Plugin loading support with filtering for PDE (1GDSC2Z)

A reminder re our discussion:; ; Vlad and myself worked through the PDE discovery of external plug-ins; (plug-ins found in the installation of an Eclipse product that will be; used to run/debug).; ; We concluded that PDE will be fine if the following conditions are met:; ; 1) There is an API on Platform similar to parsePlugins that can be; pointed to the Eclipse installation and will return a PluginRegistryModel; that includes installation filtering step. Basically; the API would answer; the following: for the input path representing the root directory/URL of; the Eclipse-based product; give me the list of plug-in models that the Core; will see when it eventually starts up the product. I will take the model;; call resolve on it and take the final list of plug-in models that will be; presented to PDE users as valid external plug-ins for making dependencies.; ; 2) When PDE comes to finally launch the run-time Eclipse; it may need to; further filter out the list of plug-ins if users turn them off in order to; override them with plug-ins in their workspace. PDE uses -plugins; <pde_working_file> for that. Vlad concluded that we need to ensure that; this option continue to work and that Core does not filter this list. You; can still resolve it; but filtering is not needed because filtering has; already been applied to creating it.; ; ; NOTES:; ; JM (5/18/2001 10:27:16 AM); 	Moving to JM for investigation; ; JM (05/06/2001 5:06:01 PM); 	Defer.

Ant: problem with the ant task and Eclipse tasks (1GIZ5Z8)

When using the ant task to execute another Ant script; if there are Eclipse tasks in this Ant script; then I get an error saying that:; BuildException: D:\Eclipse_self-hosting\0.132\eclipse\workspace\Ant-Task-Tests\InitTemplate.xml:6: Could not create task of type: initTemplate. Common solutions are to use taskdef to declare; ; To reproduce the case:; ************************; 1. Here is the first Ant script:; ; <?xml version=1.0?>; <project name=test basedir=C:\temp default=Ant>;   <target name=Ant>; 	<ant antfile=InitTemplate.xml dir=D:\Eclipse_self-hosting\0.132\eclipse\workspace\Ant-Task-Tests/>;   </target>; </project>; ; 2. Here is the second Ant script that is called: (initTemplate is a PDE Ant task.); <?xml version=1.0?>; <project name=test basedir=D:\Fab\ default=init>;   <target name=init>;     <initTemplate/>;   </target>; </project>; ; 3. Run the first script: you get the error.; ; ; ; NOTES:; ; FB (24/08/2001 2:13:47 PM); ; The EclipseAnt class; which is the one called for the ant task; creates a Project project while initializing itself. It sets the properties of the calling project to this new project.; That means that if you have put somewhere initTemplate in the first Ant script; then it works.; Try the following example that works:; ; <?xml version=1.0?>; <project name=test basedir=C:\temp default=Ant>;   <target name=Fake>;     <initTemplate/>;   </target>;   <target name=Ant>; 	<ant antfile=InitTemplate.xml dir=D:\Eclipse_self-hosting\0.132\eclipse\workspace\Ant-Task-Tests/>;   </target>; </project>; ; When the EclipseProject is created for this script; the taskdef for initTemplate is added to the taskdef list of the project. (cf. EclipseProject#createTask). Therefore; ; when the ant task creates a Project project and sets the properties of the calling project to this project; the definition of initTemplate is passed; and there is no error.; ; ** So the problem comes from the fact that when creating a task; an EclipseProject knows that it has to look into the AntPlugin task extensions list to find the class that ; ** corresponds to a given task name; but a basic Apache Project does not know how to do that.; ** Or; the ant task creates an Apache Project.; ; FB (28/08/2001 12:26:30 PM); 	Bug Fixed. Needs reviewing and releasing.; ; DJH (9/6/01 3:23:34 PM); 	Moving to DJH for confirmation/action/closure.

Plugin jar caching (1GITMTY)

Our classloader mechanisms have changed to resolve platform: URLs prior to constructing the loader (for improved performance).; 	As a side-effect; this causes the caching support built into the platform: support for remotely installed plugins to be ; 	bypassed. Need to fix for 2.0 if we want to enable running-from-server.; ; 	We should probably use an approach that; 	* passes the classpath as platform: URLs to PluginClassLoader and PlatformClassLoader; 	* construct DelegatingClassLoader (superclass) with null classpath; 	* on first use of loader; poof up the classpath doing asLocalURL() and super.addURL(...); ; 	This will trigger caching for remote jars; but delay this to first use (rather than loader construction). The current code does resolve() prior ; 	to loader construction/ use.; ; NOTES:

Eclipse ant tasks can not be used in an ant file called from another one. (1GIV8HY)

Eclipse tasks are not recognised by the ant plugin if used in an ant file that is called from another ant file. ; ; STEPS:; 1. create a test project called myProject; 2. in another project create a test.xml file with the following ant script:; 	---cut here---; <?xml version=1.0?>; <project default = main>; 	<target name=main >; 	; <refreshLocal resource=myProject />		; 		; 	</target>; </project>; 	---cut here---; ; 3. If you run this ant file; you will see that it really refreshes the myProject resource.; ; 4. Now try creating another xml file that refers to test.xml with the following:; 	---cut here---; <?xml version=1.0?>; <project default = main>; 	<target name=main >; 	; 	<ant antfile=test.xml />	; 	; 	</target>; </project>; 	---cut here---; ; 5. when you try to run this you will get the following error:; BuildException: D:\eclipseBuildWizard\plugins\AntTestProject\test.xml:5: Could not create task of type: refreshLocal. Common solutions are to use taskdef to declare your task; or; if this is an optional task; to put the optional.jar in the lib directory of your ant installation (ANT_HOME)..; BuildException: D:\eclipseBuildWizard\plugins\AntTestProject\test.xml:5: Could not create task of type: refreshLocal. Common solutions are to use taskdef to declare your task; or; if this is an optional task; to put the optional.jar in the lib directory of your ant installation (ANT_HOME)..; BuildException: D:\eclipseBuildWizard\plugins\AntTestProject\test.xml:5: Could not create task of type: refreshLocal. Common solutions are to use taskdef to declare your task; or; if this is an optional task; to put the optional.jar in the lib directory of your ant installation (ANT_HOME)..; ; NOTES:; perhaps this might be related with the PR:; 1GITPTB: ITPPDE:ALL - can not call a build.xml file that PDE has created for a component; from another ant script

Incorrect extension point docs (1GILWN6)

from eclipse.org news group; ; Subject: ;              PDE Error on Nature definition;         Date: ;              Tue; 14 Aug 2001 15:54:45 -0400;        From: ;              Pat McCarthy <patmc@us.ibm.com>;  Organization: ;              OTI-RTP - Eclipse Jumpstart Team;  Newsgroups: ;              eclipse.tools; ; ; ; ; Im not sure this was reported before - I hope not (as it just spent a bit of time hitting the wall on this). ; ; (An aside - what will it take to get this newsgroup accessible from a dejanews/google search?  Then it would be; easy to find any related entries). ; ; The PDE support for a nature extension creates the following xml: ; <extension ;       id=com.ibm.wsweducation.solution.resources.natures ;       name=eduNature ;       point=org.eclipse.core.resources.natures> ;    <nature> ;       <runtime ;             class=com.ibm.wsweducation.solution.resources.ProjectNature> ;       </runtime> ;    </nature> ; </extension> ; ; This nature could never be added to a project description - a CoreException was thrown. ; I kept replacing my nature with the JavaNature defined as part of the JDT plugin - and my add nature logic would; work fine....  I finally saw why.  This is the xml for the JDT JavaNature extension: ; <extension ;          point=org.eclipse.core.resources.natures ;          id=javanature ;          name=Java Nature> ;          <runtime> ;               <run class=org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.JavaProject> ;               </run> ;          </runtime> ; </extension> ; ; And no   <nature></nature> tags.  When I use the source view for the plug-in editor to fix my ProjectNature xml; definition (added <run></run> and removed <nature></nature>) - my nature works fine... ; ; Regards; ; ; Pat Mc. ; ; ; NOTES:; ; DG (8/16/2001 5:45:43 PM); The problem is with the incorrect nature extension point schema that was created using incorrect; reference document. ; ; Extension point schema has been fixed. Need to fix the reference document ; (org_eclipse_core_resources_natures.html in doc directory).

Deadlock in class loader (1GIZB5U)

A thread is created during the initialization of our plugin. When the thread in run; it references class that; have not yet been loaded. Both the main thread and our VCM thread are loading classes and end up in; a deadlock. To workaround we created dummy instances of certain classes before starting the thread. This; seems to force all class loading to the main thread such that when the thread runs; the classes it requires; are already loaded.; ; NOTES:; ; Jean-Michel (8/24/2001 4:05:30 PM); 	I have a zip file of the workspace that you can use to duplicate the deadlock.

IBM JDK 1.3.0 SR 9a fails with space in path (1GIRH9B)

JW(8/20/2001 10:35:20 AM):; Using the latest IBM JDK (1.3.0 Service Release 9a cn130-20010609) from Hursley fails. You cant bring up the; workbench if there is a space somewhere in the path. For example; if Eclipse is in a directory; like  c:\some path with a space\eclipse it wont come up.; ; A little bit of debugging shows that in Main.getBootPath(String) this statement:; ; 	url = getClass().getProtectionDomain().getCodeSource().getLocation(); ; Returns a url with the spaces turned into %20. For example; the above path; would become:; ; c:\some%20path%20with%20a%20space\eclipse; ; It then cant find anything because %20 is not treated as a space but as; characters in the path and it cant find that path.; ; The same thing happens in InternalBootLoader.getInstallURL().; ; A workaround for now is to use the option:; ;    -boot c:\some path with a space\eclipse; ; This then works and you can bring up Eclipse.; ; ; NOTES:

Ant: the parsing of the command line doesnt work properly if we specify a property into quotes (1GIZD8X)

We parse the command line to check if the user has specifed targets. Those targets can have names with space; so we tokenize the; command line with quotes.; But properties can be strings with space for instance. Therefore; the following command line doesnt work under Eclipse; even if it is correct:; 	-DmyProperty=Hello World; This should set the property variable ${myProperty} to Hello World. But instead of that; the UI thinks that it is a target name.; ; Interestingly; the following line works:; 	-DmyProperty Hello World; ; NOTES:; ; FB (24/08/2001 4:50:01 PM); 	Have to modify the code that parses the command line.; 	It is in AntLaunchWizard#createArgumentsArray.; ; FB (27/08/2001 2:56:12 PM); 	Bug fixed. Need to be reviewed and released.; ; DJH (9/6/01 3:23:34 PM); 	Moving to DJH for confirmation/action/closure.

HTTPClient redirects POST actions (1GJP1U9)

When an HTTP client receives a status response in the range 300 - 399 (inclusive);     it always attempts to redirect the request (as long as the number of redirects are within;     the allowed range).; ;     However; this conflicts with the HTTP spec (RFC2616) and causes problems with certain;     server applications (such as source forge).; ;    Specifically the specification states in section 10.3:; ; 		The action required MAY be carried out by the user agent without interaction; 	    with the user if and only if the method used in the second request is; 	    GET or HEAD. ; ;    There is also a specific note for 303 that a POST is redirected as a GET request to;    facilititate login screens.  There is another interesting note that 302 responses are;   erroneously treatede as 303 responses by allmost all existing HTTP clients and that;   servers may rely on this erroneous behavoiur.  (This is the case with SourceForge which;   responds to a Login POST with a 302 and expects the client to do a redirected GET at the;   provided address.

Need to have the equivalent of Plugin.find(...) but without plugin activation (1GJ8B1Q)

Plugin.find(...) can be used to locate NL variants of non-Java resources (eg. html files).; 	This is OK for most uses; but not for Workbench use; because the current call causes; 	plugin activation (because you need to get a plugin object for this). Should have this; 	call on PluginDescriptor instead.; ; ; NOTES:

Import/Export projects (1GIEJTL)

Having read a post (Project Metadata) in the newsgroups; it sounds like a good idea to have; the projects as isolated pieces as much as we can. This isolation could impact the way we; persist a projects metadata; since resources are already separate.; 	This isolation would be linked to two more APIs for IWorkspace:; 		- import(InputStream project); 		- export(OutputStream project); 	Two suggested solutions:; 	1) Separate the metadata on disk; 	2) Separate on-the-fly when exporting; ; 	It sounds good when the user wants to share projects but not necessarily create a version; of it on a VCM repository. Another advantage would be the possibility of exporting the local history; as well.; ; NOTES:

There is a fundamental problem with; 	the J9 random number generator not generating the same sequence of numbers as the JDK version.; 	As a result; the decryption of the keyring fails if the keyring was written with a different class lib.; ; DS (8/17/01 10:22:29 AM); 	I switch back and forth between JDK and J9 all the time.; ; JM (8/17/2001 12:06:56 PM); 	This is a known problem.  For now we cant do anything about it since it is actually a JCL problem that ; 	cannot be fixed (they are not allowed to look at the Sun implementation to duplicate the sequence). ; 	The workaround is to delete the .keyring file when you change between VMs.  In the furture we will ; 	probably have our own random implementation.; ; 	Moving to the 2.0 stream; ; JohnA (10/3/2001 5:28:02 PM); 	I have written an implementation of the SHA pseudo-random number generator (SHAPRNG).; 	Despite the fancy name and overwhelming acronym; the implementation is trivial (two short methods).; 	We can use this to maintain consistency across class libraries.  J9 doesnt seem to be available in; 	current builds; so Im waiting until its possible to test the implementation.  We should also auto-delete; 	the keyring file if there was a load failure; otherwise the user will have to delete it manually when we switch; 	to the new algorithm.; ; JohnA (10/9/2001 2:00:11 PM); 	See also 1GLBU8U: ITPCORE:WINNT - UTFDataFormatException opening a 202a workspace with 203; 	Different stack trace; but also likely due to the corrupted cipher stream.

[osgi] allow external libs onto the platform classpath? (1GFLHIN)

There are usecases whre the user has some Java library installed on their ; machine (e.g.; JDBC); for which they do not have a plugin.  Creating a plugin is difficult as the ; install location will be; different for all machines.  Since these libs come as part of some other ; install we cannot mandate; that they be in any particular location; form; ...; ; The standard Jvaa answer is to put these libs on the app classpath and be done ; with it. We drop the ; app classloader when we run the platform.  ; ; Alternatives for users are :; 	- create a plugin; 	- add the lib to their plugin; 	- add the lib to the bootpath; ; Alternatives for us; 	- leave the app class loader as the parent of the bootloader; 	- make some mechanism for people to add libs to the platform path; 	- allow users to spec external libs on their plugin classpath; 	- use environment vars on the classpath in a plugin; 		e.g; library name=$INSTALL$/lib.jar; 		where $INSTALL$ is an actual OS environment var which ids the ; install locatoin of the ; 		external product.  This would be ignored if the lib.jar was not ; found (as per normal class; 		path processing); ; ; NOTES:; ; JM (6/27/2001 6:46:57 PM); 	This requirement surfaced in the news group.  Basically with our ; current structure; if people are not; 	willing to supply plugins; eclipse developers cannot use the code.  ; There are cases where you can; 	create the plugin for them but there are clearly cases where people are ; expecting their own install ; 	structure.  ; ; ; ; ARCH (7/11/01 10:11:59 AM); 	Investigate but at the moment not required for 1.0

[resources] CoreFileSystemLibrary.isReadOnly() implemented in pure Java is not precise (1GI9BUE)

The CoreFileSystemLibrary.isReadOnly() method implemented in pure Java; (no core library available) is not precise enough. It uses the java.io.File.canWrite; method. For example; this method also returns false if the file name size is longer; than the permited one.; 	This question raised when running Eclipse on Neutrino. The message that showed; up in the dialog was that the file could not be written because it was read-only but; the real reason was that the file path was too long.; ; NOTES:

Need support for specifying the locale info in Plugin.find() (1GGPF9N)

Currently one can pass a path pattern $nl$ to the Plugin.find() method to get plugin resources ; for the current locale. However; it is not possible to ask for plugin resources corresponding to ; a locale different than the current one. The $nl$ pattern should perhaps be extends with some locale; info; something like ; -  $nl$ : use default locale; -  $nl:en_US$ : lookup in the en_US; then en; then try default.; ; This is needed in the help system to support a remote installation scenario (docs on a server).; ; NOTES:

The ant.jar included with the antsupport plugin does NOT include the standard Ant optional tasks.; As a result; these tasks are not available to Ant support plugin users.  ; ; NOTES:; ; JM(7/18/2001 7:00:16 PM); 	The immediate problem is easy to fix.  Include the require jar(s) in the Ant support plugin.; ; 	This does not however solve the class lookup problem.  for example; the Junit task will ; 	want to find various junit classes from the junit.jar (supplied separately).  The ant support ; 	plugin has some rudimentary support for registering various classes in an extension point ; 	and then tasks can indirect off the extension to load the actual class without requiring the ; 	supplying plugin to be a prereq of the ant plugin.  Unfortunatlye; this requires changes to ; 	the ant task (e.g.; <junit>).; ; 	I don;t know the real answer right now.  I suspect that for 1.0 the best we can do is solve ; 	this with the other third party jar problems and let people spec the jars in the ; 	java.exe -classpath arg (currently we drop that classpath and construct our own based on the ; 	plugin.xmls). Should handle empty files on restore (1GFQASF)

We should be able to handle empty files when we are; 	restoring the workspace. For instance; if we crash in the; 	middle of writing out the workspace files; (or markers; 	or syncinfo or...) then the files will have length 0 when; 	we try to read them in during the #restore.; ; NOTES:; ; JM (6/26/2001 3:54:28 PM); 	Are you saying we dont?  What are the steps to recreate this? ; 	What is the failure mode?  More info please.; ; GWG (6/26/2001 5:35:51 PM); 	Actually DJ logged this while visiting my office last week; so he can give more details.; From what I remember we crashed somehow (possibly by my own doing; not the platforms);; and when we restarted there was a problem.  DJ; do you remember more details?; ; DJH (6/26/01 7:11:46 PM); 	I believe that the scenerio was that a full_save on exit was interrupted (process ; 	killed?) and never completed. This left an empty file where we were writing since ; 	the stream was never closed. When the workspace is restored; the restore code; 	tries to read an int to get the version number for the reader and it fails with an; 	EOFException which is wrapped in a ResourceException. Optimization summary (1GHFO1M)

Summary of optimization changes:; ; Core Resources:; ; - changed tree lookups to call IPath.segment() instead of IPath.segments(); to ; avoid creating string arrays.; - DataTreeLookup objects now stored in a pool and dispensed via a factory ; method.  ; 	- Note: this currently relies on the assumption that there will never ; be more than 100 simultaneous tree readers.; 		If there are > 100 readers doing a lookup at exactly the same ; time; they may get corrupted results (since the; 		DataTreeLookup objects will start to be reused).  This seems to ; be a minimal risk; but the pool size could easily; 		be bumped up if needed...; - created a specialized structure for storing the Node ID maps during delta ; computation.  Prevents the need to create; 	instances of the Long class to use as keys.; - optimized ResourceDelta iteration to not create unnecessary ArrayList and ; Object[] instances during iteration.; - rearranged delta computation so that ResourceDelta tree is built first; and ; then moves are calculated and applied.; 	This avoids needing to create the IPath objects to walk the tree; since ; the paths held onto by the ResourceDelta; 	instances can be reused.; - optimized FileSystemResourceManager.locationFor to create less path objects.; - now passing the location as parameter to FileSystemResourceManager.write; (IFile; ...); which avoids having; to compute the location twice during file creation.; - added a singleton 8K buffer field to the FileSystemStore class; to avoid ; buffer creations for each call to transferStreams.; 	The block that uses the buffer is synchronized; to prevent multiple ; concurrent writes from attempting to use the; 	same buffer.  Since multiple concurrent writes are not currently ; supported anyway; this is just a safety measure.; - Refresh local: storing the location filesystem names in the UnifiedTree; to ; prevent having to recompute it in; 	the refresh visitor.  This drastically reduces the number of String ; creations and OS calls during the refresh operation.; - optimized the Container.getFile and Container.getFolder methods to create ; less paths.; - Marker attributes are now stored in a specialized Map structure that is ; optimized for a small set of attributes; where; 	the keys are interned Strings; so that lookup can use fast == for ; search and retrieval.; - Optimized the search for case variants during resource creation ; (Resource.findExistingResourceVariants).  Added; 	a method to ElementTree to retrieve the names of child nodes without ; having to create path objects.  Drastically; 	reduced the number of IPath creations during any operation that created ; resources.; - Removed code that was cloning the SyncInfo table each time more sync info was ; added.  This clone was unnecessary.; - Added a singleton ResourceStatus.OK.  Most operations were creating thousands ; of identical ResourceStatus instances; 	that had OK severity.  Changed code throughout resources plugin to use ; this singleton whereever possible.; - Optimized ResourceInfo lookups (Workspace.getResourceInfo); to avoid throwing ; and catching exceptions whenever; 	there was a false lookup.  Avoids the garbage and speed hit of creating ; exceptions and all the strings they contain.; - Removed superfluous Path creation in WorkspaceRoot.getProject.; ; Core Runtime:; ; - Reimplemented Path.java to be represented by an array of segments rather than ; a single string.; ; NOTES:; ; JohnA (8/27/2001 10:44:44 AM); 	Another round of optimizations that added small improvement to build ; times;; 	and huge improvements to import and deletion times.  All timings after ; this; 	point include the following extra optimizations:; ; 	- Added code in CoreFileSystemLibrary to used the default platform ; encoding when converting; 		strings to bytes for the getStat method. This avoided the ; creation of CharToByte conversion; 		objects and byte[] objects.; 	- MarkerManager.basicRemoveMarkers().  This method was opening a layer ; in the element tree; 		even if there were no markers to remove.  Optimized so that ; tree only gets opened if there; 		will actually be removals.  Big improvement to overall delete ; performance (>300%).; 	- Optimized the Resource.countResources() method to avoid creating all ; the resources it was counting.; 	- Added a hashmap to WorkspaceRoot that holds onto known project ; handles (keyed by name).  This was a big; 		improvement because project handles were created quite often ; just to find out the project location.; ; ; JohnA (8/24/2001 1:29:17 PM); 	More performance results; this time for large imports and deletes.; 	The sample set was a wad of eclipse source; 5621 files; 497 folders.; ; JohnA (8/27/2001 11:44:26 AM); 	Redid some of the build tests to find out effect of further ; optimizations.; 	This is the same test as the build tests above; running on IBM JDK.; 	Comparison is between HEAD (no optimizations); and all cumulative ; optimizations.;

Strings contributed by fragment.xml cannot be translated (1GHOZI1)

The current fragment support does not allow strings in the fragment.xml to be isolated and translated. In particular; this is a problem for ; 	any contributed extensions (but also for extension point names/ etc that appear in PDE UI).; ; NOTES:; 	The problem is that we only support a single plugin.properties; and information contributed by fragments is looked up in the; 	plugin.properties bundle. Any one given fragment could play the NL game and supply plugin.properties containing base strings plus; 	strings for itself; but this would miss other fragments.; ; 	We need a mechanism that allows a fragment to contribute strings that logically augment the plugin.properties; without creating; 	conflicts with the base plugin; or other fragments for that plugin (ie. sort-of-a fragmented bundle). Until we have a solution;; 	fragments are really not usable to contribute extensions that need to be NL-enabled. ; ; 	Related PR: 1GHH215: ITPCORE:WINNT - translated plugin.properties cannot be contributed by fragments

Misc performance issues (1GHH6WS)

findExistingResourceVariants is expensive (16% of all string allocations for a typical op;; 22% of total time for a large import).  In one import case this was over 45% of string allocations.; 	- perhaps need methods for performing a case insensitive search in the tree; ; Sync Info; 	- consider a more specialized structure than hashmap; possibly optimizing the common case of only one sync info; ; Markers; 	- Java core is holding onto marker deltas.  JavaModelManager.fDeltaProcessor.fCurrentDelta refers to old; 		marker deltas long after the operation is finished.  This can transitively hold onto a lot of garbage.; 	- marker serialization could improve space/performance by canonicalizing marker attribute keys; 		before writing.  That is; build a string table; and just serialize offsets in the string table.  This would; 		be a great savings because most markers have the same attributes.; ; ; NOTES:

translated plugin.properties cannot be contributed by fragments (1GHH215)

PluginDescriptor.gerResourceBundle(Locate) is not fragment-aware (only looks inside the ; 	plugin directory). Consequently; the NL versions of the plugin.properties files cannot be; 	contributed by fragments.; ; NOTES:; ; JM (7/26/2001 10:04:07 AM); 	Shouldnt getResourceBundle() use the same classpath as the normal plugin class loader?; 	Actually it is unclear why we need a different class loader at all?  the current code limits us to ; 	finding resource bundles only in the install dir of the plugin.  Bundles in jars and in library; 	directories will not be found.; ; VK (7/26/01 10:20:44 AM); 	I think where we are right now is a statement of how we evolved. The code predates fragments; 	and was there so workbench could get at translated plugin.properties; or any other resource; 	bundle that was NOT part of the actual code .jar. None of our plugins specify <library name=.//>; 	so the extra class loader was just a cheap way (in terms of coding) of getting the plugin.properties; 	found and loaded. So the question is; what are the correct semantics to support? Some possible; 	choices:; 	(1)	use extra loader; make its classpath include plugin dir; and all detected fragment dirs. This; 			would be morally equivalent to the current support. One would place plugin.properties; 			files in the root of the plugin or fragment directory; 	(2)	defer to the plugin loader to do the loading. This would require all plugins to specify; 			<library name=.//> so the default plugin.properties are found.; 	(3)	use extra loader as today; but parent it using the plugin loader (rather than null).; 			This would preserve current behavior (ie. not have the downside of (2)); but if we fail; 			would then do the regular search (fragment aware). The fragments would have a choice; 			of specifying <library name=.//> and exposing the property file; or could just; 			bundle it at a root of one of the supplied jars.; 	Would need the <library name=.//> handling fixed (as file:; not jar:).; 	I think I would vote for (3) ... ; ; VK (7/26/01 10:43:21 AM); 	On second thought; (3) would cause the parent loader to be searched first before going to the ; 	base plugin directory; so this may not be what we want. So; 	(4)	use extra loader; its classpath set to plugin directory + classpath of plugin loader; ; VK (7/26/01 11:09:33 AM); 	Have tried with option (3) as the fix and things seem to work. ; ; 	Looking at the trace; option (3) causes the classloaders for ALL plugins that contribute to the UI to be created ; 	as part of the workbench startup. This is because the workbench is looking for the plugin.properties strings; 	and option (3) causes the plugin loaders to be created. This may be OK (not a heavy operation) but some of the ; 	plugin loaders may never be actually used beyond this point. Also; when searching for the bundles; option (3); 	causes the prereq loader trees to be searched (search is guaranteed to fail for plugin.properties bundles); ; VK (7/27/01 11:56:41 AM); 	Have done a few unscientific measurements using option (3) [the simplest fix]. All are done using the following configuration:; 		slow machine: PII-266; 		Win-NT; 		launching self-hosted eclipse; base drop + example plugins; 		running from jars (not dev mode); 		running with -debug; but only runtime tracing enabled (to get timings); 		2 perspectives open on startup (resource; scripting); ; 		Case 1: drop 0.127; 												1			2			3			4			5			Average (ms); 			Startup complete:	24925	24936	24916	24956	24876		24922; ; 		Case 2: drop 0.127 + change for option (3); ; 			Startup complete:	26088	26150	26138	26077	26098		26110; ; 		So it would appear the startup cost of creating all the loaders that contrinuted UI elements; PLUS performing ; 		longer lookup searches for the plugin.properties bundles is about 1 second on my slow machine. ; 		This is with the base drop plus examples. In a large configuration we are likely to have 2-5 times as many plugins; 		contributing to the UI so we would see corresponding increase in startup times.; ; VK (7/30/01 3:03:18 PM); 	Option (4) comes out marginally slower than current support ... basically not paying the performance cost of option (3).; 	The conclusion is that the actual extra cost is not from creation of the loaders; but rather from the unnecessary; 	searching down the prerequisite tree (always guaranteed to fail for the bundle; is multipled by Java locale-based; 	qualified name search). ; ; VK (7/30/01 11:47:39 AM); 	Have created 1GHOOPO: ITPCORE:WINNT - translated plugin.properties (R1.0 support) to track the R1.0 partial fix.; 	Using option (4) as the fix. Keeping this PR open to make sure we revisit this in the 2.0 stream.

UndoUnzip can remove files that were on filesys in the first place (1GENCWR)

When an installation (unzip) has failed; the undo code will remove every file defined in the jars ; install.xml and inside the jar.   This means that if a plugin was previously installed by another ; component; and a new component is being installed containing the same plugin; and the installation; failed; the undo code will remove that plugin.  ; ; The undo code will need to be smart enough to remove whats actually unzipped; OR; we do all the; unzip in a staging area and move things to the actual location when everythings good (theres still; the chance of failure during the move; though).; ; NOTES:

Install into Linux shared space (1GIRJ5G)

from eclipse.org; ; Subject: ;              Re: Multiple users sharing an Eclipse install on Linux;         Date: ;              Thu; 16 Aug 2001 16:37:33 -0400;        From: ;              Steve Francisco <cisco@ca.ibm.com>;  Organization: ;              EclipseCorner;  Newsgroups: ;              eclipse.tools;   References: ;              1 ; 2; ; ; Thanks Vlad; thats good news.  I believe it is reasonable to have software; updates installable only by someone with root authority.  Theoretically it; should be possible to have Help > Software Update work; but prompt the user for; the root password for proper authentication.  Ive seen this done when you use; RPM and arent logged in as root.; -- Steve; ; Vlad Klicnik wrote:; ; > Steve; ability to share a single install was one of the base design points; > of Eclipse (this is one place you can say weve learned from our prior; > experiences ;-) ). We have taken great care to ensure plugins dont rely on; > having write access to the installation tree. In fact we have made it hard; > for a plugin to know where exactly it is installed (all normal access is; > through URLs using an eclipse protocol that does the magic mapping). So as; > long as you launch the shared Eclipse with each user having their own; > writeable workbench (tool state and actual resources) all should be well; > (as you are finding out).; >; > There may be cases where a plugin was written to rely on accessing its; > directory. We do not make it easy to program this way; so this scenario; > will be a case of someone consciously going out and programming around the; > APIs we provide (I am mentioning this only because there are several; > discussion threads in the newsgroup with questions along these lines).; > These plugins would fail in your configuration.; >; > The only other thing I can think of is that the Help> Software Update; > function will be disabled for your users (cannot install/ update into; > shared r/o tree). Updates to the shared install would need to be done by a; > user with r/w access doing the update (using native installer or the update; > support).; ; ; ; ; NOTES:

Performance: slow autobuild with many projects (1GL4NI5)

From EC (Stef van Dijk):; ; We have someone here who is moving a large number of projects from VAME to; Eclipse. Were talking about over 150 projects which are loaded into the; workspace at one time. Note that this is actually a normal configuration for; the type of work this person does. Having moved these projects; we are now; seeing a performance issue we dont quite understand.; Every time a file is changed; a progress dialog appears with an updating; message which says Reading resource change information followed by the; names of all the projects in the workspace. This dialog lasts about 20-30; seconds. Now if we were changing java source and there were numerous; interrelated projects I could understand; but that does not appear to be the; case.; ; For example; we created a brand new java project which has no references to; any of the other 150+ projects (nor do the other projects reference this new; one). We then created an .xml file in this project and opened it in an; editor. Everytime we save the .xml file; we see the progress dialog racing; through all the other projects in the workspace. Why is this? None of those; projects should be affected by this project; and certainly not by a change; to an .xml file.; ; Then Philippe said:; ; The Reading resource change information message indicates the Java; builder is asking the platform for the delta computations. ; ; When the build button is pressed; all Java projects are traversed to see; if they need to be rebuild based on the changes in them and the changes in; their prerequisites. Note that a change inside a resource file on the; classpath still need the Java builder to copy it into the binary output; location.; ; ; NOTES:; ; JohnA (10/5/2001 5:20:45 PM); 	A massive improvement here could be accomplished as follows:; ; 		- find out the set of projects that have changed; 		- for each builder; if any of its interesting projects have changed; invoke builder; ; 	This would mean we dont even have to invoke the builder if we know it wont; 	be interested in any changes.

Startup discovery of more than one version of conflicting component (1GET5H8)

VK (6/4/01 1:58:05 PM); 		If on startup we discover more than one version of the same component and they all conflict with some already installed; 		configuration; we only process the most recent version of the conflicting component (the registry model filters out; 		all but one version of the component; so we do not even see the others).; ; 		This is not correct during startup discovery; because we need to inactivate all conflicts (components and their plugins and; 		fragments).; ; NOTES:

Refactoring: operation arent cancelable since the cant be executed in a different thread. (1GEJ61L)

All refactorings arent cancelable right now since that arent executed in a ; separate thread. They have to be executed in the UI thread; 	since they are updating documents which trigger events to the editor; ; which updates the UI.; ; 	Possible soultion is to used a special progress monitor that reads the ; event loop on every tick. This would make it possible to ; 	cancel an operation even if it is executed in the UI thread.; ; ; 	See: 1GEJ66W: ITPUI:WIN2000 - Need way to cancel an operation that ; doesnt specify fork= true.; ; NOTES:; ; DB (31.07.2001 11:34:49); 	We already have changed the implementation so that a refactoring is ; cancelable during its precondition checking phase. It is not; 	cancelable when we the actual code change takes place. In my opinion ; this is the right way to go anyway since:; ; 		- the precondintion checking takes the longest amount of time.; 		- the actual change shouldnt be cancelable anyway since the ; program can end up in an inconsistent state. Furthermore it is not; 		  possible to generate an undo object for an canceled ; refactoring.; 		- the user can undo the refactoring by activating undo.; ; 	Opt to close the PR.; ; 	Waiting for comments from EG and AK.; ; DB (31.07.2001 16:13:08); 	Not fully true. Precondition checking is only cancelable when pressing ; Next on the first page. When pressing Finish the whole; 	operation isnt cancelable at all.; ; 	There are different solutions for that problem:; 	; 	a.) we post every change to a document into the UI thread. This enables ; to run the operation in a different thread and makes it; 	     cancelable.; 	b.) the document / document provider or the listener fixes the problem. ; But then JFace Text is the only component that deals with; 	     multi thread issues.; 	c.) Java Core improves IBuffer in a way that it reports more fine ; grained changes. This would enable the document to keep markers; 	     for non changed text segements. If we do that; the Refactoring ; Core could use IBuffer from Java Core to do their text changes instead; 	     of IDocument.; ; 	I opt to do c.).

API: Subclassing MarkerRulerAction (1GGLSQP)

org.eclipse.ui.texteditor.MarkerRulerAction is documented as not meant to be subclass in the API documentation.; However it is subclassed in org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.javaeditor.BreakpointRulerAction.  Additionally; it appears that; MarkerRulerAction defines some useful base functionality so that it is possible to get a working breakpoint ruler; action for a simple language plugin just by overriding addMarker.; ; SUGGESTED CHANGES:; One of the following:; 1. Revise the API documentation to describe MarkerRulerAction as subclassable; 2. Refactor MarkerRulerAction into AbstractMarkerRulerAction; leaving out addMarker to be defined by subclasses.; Make a new MarkerRulerAction as a subclass of AbstractMarkerRulerAction.  Document AbstractMarkerRulerAction; as subclassable but MarkerRulerAction as non-subclassable.; ; DS (7/10/01 1:37:25 PM); 	Sent email to KH.; ; DW (7/18/01 8:52:50 AM); 	moved to JUI.

Simplify marker rendering (1GI8NXV)

From EC:; ; I assume that you talk about the breakpoints view contributed; by the org.eclipse.debug.ui plugin. The mechanisms how the vertical; ruler and the breakpoints view look up icons differ. The breakpoints; view has specific knowledge about markers representing breakpoints;; whereas the vertical ruler does not assume a specific marker type.; ; If you create a marker which should be rendered independently of; its state; the following is recommended: You specify a particular icon; for a particular marker type in your plugin.xml file; e.g.:; <extension point=org.eclipse.ui.markerImageProviders>;     <imageprovider  id=myplugin.myMarkerProvider;         markertype=myplugin.mymarker;         icon=myiconlocation/mymarkericon.gif>;     </imageprovider>; </extension>; ; Your definition is picked up by all editors relying on the provided; default implementation.; ; If  you create a marker that should be rendered depending on; its state; you have to provide an Annotation which masters the; required rendering. In addition; you have to make sure that for; your marker your custom annotation is created. If you deal with; your own IAnnotationModel; it depends on your implementation; how your annotation is created and added to the  model. If you; use; e.g.; the ResourceAnnotationModel; you can create a subclass; of MarkerAnnotation; say MyMarkerAnnotation; and subclass; ResourceAnnotationModel overriding createMarkerAnnotation(IMarker); to return MyMarkerAnnotation. Thus; when building an editor; you have; to configure it with document providers which return your custom; ResourceAnnotationModel. As example look at JDT. They provide their; own JavaMarkerAnnotation; annotation model; etc.; We are looking into simplifying this for the future.; ; NOTES:

Visual Coordination: Scrollbar and Combo Arrowbutton (1FSGOQU)

AW (23.03.00 20:10:27); 	SWT 0.39:; 	While trying to beautify VAME for Motif Ive got the impression that the Motif implementation; 	(for obvious reasons) lacks some coordination between the visual elements.; ; 	Coordination means: It should be possible to compose larger controls from single controls without; 	having to know how a specific control looks on every platform. This requires careful upfront tuning of sizes; borders; gaps; 	so that things line up nicely; double borders are avoided etc. and this all without too many tweaks in client code.; ; 	A classical example is how to align a label and a button so that the text baselines; 	line up independent of the size of both controls and the fonts used.; ; 	Im not asking for all that right now; but it would be nice if we can state some basic invariants;; 	which are independent from a platform.; 	; AW (27.03.00 11:02:07); 	See first: 1FSA2CS: SWT:DCR - Visual coordination of SWT controls!; ; 	Width and horizontal position of vertical scrollbar matches size and position of arrowbutton in Combo.; 	(See top left Pane in LFs Repository browser); ; 	True?; ; SN (3/27/00 10:33:43 AM); 	FYI: The drop down arrow and scroll bar arrow in Windows are different bitmaps; 	and have been different sizes and looked different in the past (Windows 3.11).; ; KH (4/11/00 9:36:42 AM); 	Added AWs comments from March 23 and moved 1FSA2CS to ZZ-Trash.; ; 	McQ (27/11/2000 3:03:29 PM) -; 		See comments in:; 			1FSGONG

Layout: Exceptions when horizontalSpan > remaining columns (1FFC6XZ)

Run the code below. ; You will get a NullPointerException because label2 spans two columns ; and there is only one column left on the first row.; If you change the span of label2 to be more than the number of columns ; in the grid layout (ie. gridData.horizontalSpan = layout.numColumns + 1;); you get an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.; ; ----------------------8<----------------------8<----------------------8<----------------------8<; Shell shell = new Shell();; Label label;; Label label2;; GridData gridData = new GridData();; GridLayout layout = new GridLayout();; ; layout.numColumns = 2;; shell.setLayout(layout);; ; label = new Label(shell; SWT.NULL);				// comment this line out and it works; label2 = new Label(shell; SWT.NULL);; gridData.horizontalSpan = layout.numColumns;; label2.setLayoutData(gridData);; ; shell.setSize(300; 100);; shell.open(); ; NOTES:; ; LK (7/15/99 4:40:55 PM); 	The layout is defined incorrectly; so this is user error.  These are the kinds; 	of things I wanted to pick up with exceptions; but SN wasnt keen on the idea of; 	introducing exceptions for incorrect layout specification.; ; KR (7/15/99 5:22:11 PM); 	The programmer should never get a NullPointerException or ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.; 	That would lead people to believe that theres a bug in the SWT layout code instead ; 	of pointing them to a mistake in their layout code.; 	If the programmer defines a layout incorrectly; a corresponding exception should ; 	be thrown. A general exception like InvalidLayoutData should be good enough.; 	An option would be to ignore the offending widget and not lay it out at all. That; 	way the programmer would see that theres something wrong with the layout spec.; 	We should sort this out when we do the SWT exception pass.; ; KH (7/20/99 9:04:53 AM); 	Moving to time-permitting; ; 	McQ (10/10/2000 12:08:31 PM) -; 		This seems like another instance of having better exception support; 		as described in 1FEA8ZE: SWT:ALL - When to throw exceptions.; 		Moving to inactive.

DCR - Reducing number of adapter classes (1FGCI1N)

AW (04.08.99 13:04:00); 	One way to reduce the number of adapter classes for typed listeners is to; 	bring down the number of methods in the corresponding listener interface to one.; 	(In this case you dont need default implementations for the methods you are not interested in;; 	so you dont need an adapter).; ; 	Folding listener methods into one especially makes sense if the typical  handler code is similar.; 	On the other hand a field has to be added to the typed event to distinguish the different cases.; 	; 	Ive looked through the listeners and found the following interfaces where the above applies IMHO: ; 		FocusListener:	focusGained/focusLost; 		KeyListener: keyPressed/keyReleased; 		MenuListener: menuHidden/menuShown ; 		SelectionListener: widgetDoubleSelected/widgetSelected; 		TreeListener: treeCollapsed/treeExpanded; ; 	By doing this it would be possible to eliminate 5 of 8 adapters.; ; NOTES:; ; SN (8/4/99 12:04:57 PM); 	One way to reduce the number of classes it to go back to untyped events.; 	This will get rid of every class.; ; 	The other thing to do is get rid of the Adaptors and make JFACE define only; 	the ones they actually use.  Obviously; if JFACE uses them all; then there; 	is no win.  I have already suggested this to Kevin.; ; SN (8/4/99 12:07:05 PM); 	Does Erich agree that these should be collapsed?; ; 	McQ (10/10/2000 12:32:39 PM) -; 		Nothing will be done about this at this time.

DCR - GridBagLayout compatible LayoutManager (1FGCPO2)

EG (04.08.99 15:44:50); 	JFace legacy provides a CellContainer that is used heavily in existing;     JFace applications. Providing something similar in JFace would simplify;     the migration to JFace2000. We have tried to implement CellContainer on top of GridLayout; but;     the models are too different and we had to give up. The major difference is that;     in CellContainer (which is based on GridBagLayout) you can position a widget;     directly by giving coordinates e.g. 3; 4 and you dont have to worry about empty cells.;     Emulating this with the GridLayout would require to insert dummy filler widgets into;     empty cells. This is clumsy and wastes resources.; ;     Notice: the priority of this PR depends on how important backward compatibility ;     with JFace legacy is.; ; NOTES:; ; 	McQ (10/10/2000 12:33:30 PM) -; 		Nothing will be done about this at this time.

Visual Coordination: Borders (1FSGONG)

AW (23.03.00 20:10:27); 	SWT 0.39:; 	While trying to beautify VAME for Motif Ive got the impression that the Motif implementation; 	(for obvious reasons) lacks some coordination between the visual elements.; ; 	Coordination means: It should be possible to compose larger controls from single controls without; 	having to know how a specific control looks on every platform. This requires careful upfront tuning of sizes; borders; gaps; 	so that things line up nicely; double borders are avoided etc. and this all without too many tweaks in client code.; ; 	A classical example is how to align a label and a button so that the text baselines; 	line up independent of the size of both controls and the fonts used.; ; 	Im not asking for all that right now; but it would be nice if we can state some basic invariants;; 	which are independent from a platform.; 	; AW (27.03.00 10:59:33); 	See first: 1FSA2CS: SWT:DCR - Visual coordination of SWT controls!; ; 	 (Tree w/o Border) inside (Composite w Border) == (Tree w Border); ; 	A borderless Tree (or Text; Table; etc.) as the single child of a Composite with border has identical; 	appearance as a Tree with border.; 		; 	True on all platforms?; 	(If not then Im in trouble because I cannot write an adorned viewer whose visual; 	appearance matches the native one; right now this seems to be true on Windows but not on Motif); ; KH (4/11/00 9:36:42 AM); 	Added AWs comments from March 23 and moved 1FSA2CS to ZZ-Trash.; ; 	McQ (27/11/2000 12:56:44 PM) -; 		To some extent; appearance differences between platforms are simply a *direct* outcome; 		of our decision to use platform widgets with as little playing with defaults as possible. ; 		We still strongly believe that as soon as you (actually; we) start changing the defaults which; 		the platform is providing for the widgets; we get in to trouble. The perfect example of this is; 		the well known widgets dont change colour when you change the Appearance control; 		panel values problem; where if you explicitely set window colors; then the o/s wont update; 		them for you when users request it.; ; 		In all honestly; we dont have time to deal with these issues in this cycle anyway.; 		Moving to inactive.

If you are running Windows 95 with ; no service packs; 		the bits are not honoured and the selection will always ; disappear.  At any rate; it; 		should only be a visual thing.  If you query the tree when it ; doesnt have focus; it; 		should still claim that the item is selected.; ; 		You need to be running the Common Controls DLL Version 4.71 (or ; greater) or all; 		sorts of things wont work.  To get this DLL; download the ; lastest IE.  IE replaces; 		all sorts of system DLLs when it installs.; ; ; EG (23.04.99 13:48:13); 	checked the DLL version it is 4.72.3609.2200; ; KH (4/23/99 11:54:58 AM); 	I tried your example and because you have set the background color to ; grey you are not seeing; 	the disabled selection in the tree. For now you should change the ; background color to any other; 	color. ; ; 	Changing this PR to be a DCR for us to investigate the possibility of ; controlling the trees disabled; 	color. ; ; SN (4/29/99 12:38:09 PM); 	There is no Windows API to do this.  Moving to fixed.; ; GDA (4/30/99 10:09:56 AM); 	To confirm; you are claiming; 		1)	There is no way to set the selection color; 		2) There is no way to set the disable selection color (other ; than a choice of show nothing (useless); show gray); 		3) There is no way to tell it to leave the selection showing ; even when the widget loses focus; ; 	Note: From ui perspective having the selection go away (as opposed to ; the gray it mode) is very bad.; 		Even the graying is misleading because it implies the object ; selection is not active when in fact; 		it actually is.; ; ; SN (4/30/99 4:14:59 PM); 	1) and 2) are correct.  3) is wrong. Extra space in table.calculateSize when scrollbar not required (1FOPZNB)

When you run the following example; you will see that after the two columns; there is some extra space in the table.; ; Example; ; 	Shell shell = new Shell ();; 	shell.setLayout(new GridLayout());; ; 	Table table = new Table(shell; SWT.NONE);; 	table.setWidths(new int[] {140; 140});; 	table.setLinesVisible(true);; 	GridData gridData = new GridData();; 	gridData.heightHint = 200;; 	table.setLayoutData(gridData);; 	; 	shell.open ();; 	; 	Display display = shell.getDisplay ();; 	while (!shell.isDisposed ()) {; 		if (!display.readAndDispatch ()) display.sleep ();; 	}; ; What is happening is as follows:; 1) Table always has the style bits SWT.H_SCROLL  and SWT.V_SCROLL set whether you set them or not:; ; 	Table::void createWidget (Composite parent) {; 		/*; 		* Feature in Windows.  It is not possible to create; 		* a table that does not have scroll bars.  Therefore;; 		* no matter what style bits are specified; set the; 		* H_SCROLL and V_SCROLL bits so that the SWT style; 		* will match the widget that Windows creates.; 		*/; 		style |= SWT.H_SCROLL | SWT.V_SCROLL;; 		...; 	}; ; 2) In computeSize (which is called by GridLayout); Table always; calculates the space needed for the scroll bars regardless of; whether they are to be displayed or not:; ; 	Table::public Point computeSize (int wHint; int hHint; boolean changed) {; 		...; 		if ((style & SWT.V_SCROLL) != 0) {; 			width += OS.GetSystemMetrics (OS.SM_CXVSCROLL);; 		}; 		if (((style & SWT.H_SCROLL) != 0) && (hHint != SWT.DEFAULT)) {; 			height += OS.GetSystemMetrics (OS.SM_CYHSCROLL);; 		}; 		...; 	}; ; I commented out the lines in computeSize that were adding the; width of the scrollbar to the total width and I did not see the extra space.; ; NOTES:; ; 	McQ (2/1/01 4:10:39 PM) -; 		VI to indicate what the right answer is.; ; 	CM (3/19/01 4:37:00 PM); 		P1/M1 (April 1st; 2001); ; 	CM (3/29/2001 11:19:19 AM)	; 		SWT always allows extra space for scrollbars that might show up;; 		even if they never show up. This is true for all widgets: List; Tree; Table; etc.; 		If we take away the extra space; a scrollbar can pop up and obscure list items;; 		and the user has no way of seeing what is under the scrollbar (i.e. items are clipped).; ; 		In most widgets; the extra space for a potential scrollbar looks fine - in fact it is what the user expects.; 		The only widget this is a problem for is Table - because Table has vertical lines between the columns;; 		and the extra space for the vertical scrollbar actually looks tacked on. i.e. it looks as if someone; 		added a skinny extra column on the right; which is suspiciously the width of a scrollbar.; ; 		Heres the problem - the native Table always provides scrollbars; whether or not they are displayed;; 		i.e. even if the application doesnt want them and doesnt put enough items in the Table to need them.; ; 		One possible (hack) solution is to keep a boolean slot that keeps track of whether or not the application; 		created the widget with VSCROLL. If they did not; we can assume that they prefer not to have the extra space.; ; 		This could cause more problems than it solves; however; and since the Eclipse team seems to have; 		found another way around the problem; this PR should go to Inactive.

getFocusControl inside FocusOut event inconsistent (1FMITIE)

If you ask the display for getFocusWindow() while inside; a callback for FocusOut; on windows you get the new window; which has gained focus; while on unix you get the old window; that is losing focus.; ; NOTES:; ; KR (5/8/00 5:34:14 PM); 	This feature just got me again. ; 	I changed the focus out listener of Tree to force an update after the selection was ; 	redrawn. ; 	This is intended to fix repaint behavior that previously left the focus item blank while ; 	the application did a time consuming operation in its own focus out listener.; 	However; the Tree does not know that it no longer has the focus because; during the ; 	repaint; getFocusControl() still returns the Tree since the focus event processing is ; 	not complete at that point.; 	Bottom line: This PR needs to be fixed.; ; SN (5/9/00 2:43:40 PM); 	This is super hard to fix without faking out the OS.  All we do is query the OS; 	so as the real focus widget at that time is different on the different platforms.; 	So; if we try and fake it; we open ourselves up for bizzare bugs and behavior; 	because we no longer match reality.; ; 	I understand why this is a problem for you.  Focus events are sent on all platforms.; 	We cannot post them because; if we wait for the event loop; youll get weird behavior; 	For example; if you open a bunch of windows and then fall back to the event loop;; 	the windows will have opened; changed focus from one to the other etc. and then youll; 	start getting the callbacks; not actually at the time that the focus was changed.; ; 	I am open to suggestions.  On possibility is to use a slot in the event to contain; 	the correct value.  BTW:  What should the value be?; ; KR (5/10/00 10:25:26 AM); 	getFocusControl() should return the widget that actually has focus at that time.; 	If Motif sends the focus out event when the old widget still has focus (ie. AboutToLooseFocus); 	then SWT would be lying if getFocusControl() returned the new focus widget. ; 	The best solution would be to do some magic so that the new widget already has focus ; 	when the FocusOut event is sent.; 	If you provide the focus widget in a slot (or somewhere else) it should be the new focus widget.; 	In the FocusOut listener you know the widget that lost focus - in most cases its the receiver.; 	(or you can check the event.widget field). Theres no other way of finding out the new focus ; 	widget 	though; so that should be provided somewhere else. It would also be consistent with ; 	the FocusIn event - both focus events would then provide the 	new focus widget.; 	You dont necessarily have to provide the new focus widget at all. You could just doc in; 	getFocusControl that the return value is platform specific if used within a focus listener.; 	I could store the focus state in a field of my own to work around this.; ; DR (5/10/00 11:56:14 AM); 	Unfortunately there is no good way for us to fix getFocusControl().  ; 	The OS will simply not provide the required information.  ; 	We will have to comment getFocusControl() that the return value is ; 	platform specific when inside a LoseFocus event.; ; 	If you are looking for a path to consistant code between ; 	the two platforms the event widget is consistant between the two platforms.  ; 	The widget in the LoseFocus event is the widget that is really losing focus; ; 	the widget in the GainFocus event is really the widget gaining focus.; ; 	SN (2/1/01 2:51:43 PM) -; 		SN to investigate further.; ; 	McQ (26/06/2001 10:03:59 AM) -; 		SN to add comments as indicated above by DR.

DCR: Tables need to support cell editing (1FGPLPP)

SWT table cells are not editable. This will be required by  SWT/JFace user over ; and over.; We need this feature and we dont want our users to reinvent it in each ; application.; ; Cell editing has to be based on the column type. It should be; possible to set cell editor as a widget that is activated and positioned over ; the; cell on editing trigger (one or two mouse clicks in the cell). SWT should ; provide; support to position given widget and make sure it tracks resize; focus and; other events. To be fully usable; it should be possible to set different; editors for each row in the column (for example; property sheet implementation; will use editors based on the property in the selected row.; ; It is desirable that this mini-framework is not bound to TableViewer in ; order to be available for dialogs and wizards as well. ; NOTES:; ; EG (12.08.99 15:12:48); 	It is important that SWT enables the cell editing in a Table. The ; mechanism;     to support this is an SWT.; 	moved to SWT; ; KH (8/12/99 9:21:14 AM); ; ColumnTypes and TypeEditors have nothing to do with widgets. Am I missing ; something? But what ; is the difference between pressing a checkbox that happens to be in a table and ; pressing a checkbox ; anywhere else in the UI? As far as the widget is concerned an event is fired in ; both cases.; ; I want to be clear about this. I acknowledge that VA2000 will have a ; requirement for property editors and ; this will have to support editing values in tables that are not strings. I ; envision having to support at ; least: buttons (of several flavors - push; check etc) ; combo boxes etc.  ; VA/Java uses this. It may be possible to implement table editing using real ; native widgets and make them appear ; to be children of the table widget but I strongly suspect that we will not be ; able to do this. You have seen ; some of the problems already with the size of the combobox. There are internal ; technical reasons why ; using native widgets causes problems as well. But you and other SWT users ; should not have to care ; about how it is implemented. The support you can expect from SWT is the ability ; to insert and remove ; widget like things in table items and receive callbacks when the user interacts ; with them. Erich and I have ; talked about this and we both agree that the enabling work to put widgets ; into the table; handle resize ; events; scrolling etc properly belongs in SWT. High level support to validate ; input; map between types ; and editors belongs in the viewers. (Erich if I have misrepresented you then ; please correct); ; KH (8/12/99 9:21:46 AM); 	; 	Changing title. Moving to time-permitting. Waiting on prioritzed list ; from customer.; ; 	McQ (10/10/2000 3:30:08 PM) -; 		We implemented a TableEditor class which provides the resizing ; and positioning; 		behavior for placing a widget over a cell in a table. We do not ; envision doing more; 		than this in this round.
